notes.note: The consular and other elections for 57 BC, expected to clear the way for a senatorial motion on Cicero's recall; held in late summer 58 BC. Cicero waits at Thessalonica for word of their outcome.

The tribunes of the plebs elected for 57 BC, not yet in office; Cicero pins his last hope on them since they could bring his recall before the people. Several (Sestius, Milo, Fadius) did fight for it.

A Greek city on the Propontis (Sea of Marmara), far from Italy; a possible retreat Cicero contemplates as hope of an early recall fades. The greater distance would slow his correspondence with Atticus.

notes.anchor: Sent on the Nones of August
notes.note: 5 August 58 BC. The Nones fell on the 5th in months of 31 days (here Sextilis, the old name for August), reckoned by the Roman calendar.
